machine.name: DC Critical Alert Flip-Flop
machine.description: RS flip-flop that latches the multi-system critical alert state. SET by DCCriticalSynthesizer [1,0] when multiple alarms coexist, RESET when all clear.

metadata.author: Reality Engine
metadata.role: Final-stage alert latch; represents the highest-severity combined failure state

singleTransitionAssumption.scope: not applicable — all event vectors are isInitial (combinatorial)
singleTransitionAssumption.notes: Both CRITICAL and ALL_CLEAR events are isInitial. Combinatorial RS flip-flop; state persists via perceptual space value retention. The [148:150] byte region holds the current critical alert state and drives external alerting systems.

metadata.description: Activates critical alert when synthesizer detects multi-system failure
metadata.trigger: DCCriticalSynthesizer MULTI_ALARM pattern fires [1,0] to bytes [72:74]

metadata.name: CRITICAL
metadata.description: Synthesizer signal [1,0]: multiple subsystems in alarm — activate critical alert
CRITICAL ALERT ACTIVE: Multi-system failure — immediate intervention required
Clears critical alert when synthesizer confirms all subsystems clear
metadata.trigger: DCCriticalSynthesizer ALL_CLEAR pattern fires [0,1] to bytes [72:74]

metadata.name: ALL_CLEAR
metadata.description: Synthesizer signal [0,1]: all subsystems normal — clear critical alert
CRITICAL ALERT CLEARED: All monitored subsystems nominal

inputSequences.name: Critical Alert Activation
inputSequences.description: All-clear to critical: multiple subsystems fail simultaneously

inputSequences.name: Critical Alert Resolution
inputSequences.description: Critical to all-clear: all subsystems recover

inputSequences.name: Critical Event Lifecycle
inputSequences.description: Full critical event: nominal → alert fires → persists → resolves

metadata.note: Alert state held by perceptual space retention; clears only when synthesizer fires [0,1]
